items.title: Moore goals after siren as Hawks and Pies settle for thrilling draw
items.summary: A goal after the siren from Dylan Moore allows Hawthorn to claim a draw against Collingwood at the MCG.

items.title: NSW strikes late to win Game I as Origin series heads to Queensland
items.summary: NSW has come from behind to win the opening match of the Women's State of Origin series, and their coach is looking forward to trying to win the shield in Queensland.

items.title: Man accused of attending Mount Gambier businesses while nude faces court
items.summary: A Mount Gambier man is granted bail after he was allegedly nude while in businesses and on a busy main road three times in the past week.

items.title: Savers Australia denies allegations of bed bugs at Brunswick thrift store
items.summary: Thrift store chain Savers Australia has denied there is evidence of bed bugs at their popular Brunswick shop, as allegations of an outbreak continue to circulate online.

items.title: Victorian bottle shops awash with illicit alcohol, researchers find
items.summary: The National Drug Research Institute found 30 per cent of bottle shops it audited across different areas of Melbourne and regional Victoria were stocking illicit alcohol.

items.title: Chalmers does little to stop capital gains tax debate as budget looms
items.summary: Seven years ago, proposed changes to capital gains tax and negative gearing were named as part of the reason Labor lost the unlosable election. Times have changed.

items.title: RACQ denies misleading customers with insurance notices amid ASIC Federal Court lawsuit
items.summary: When the corporate regulator accused RACQ of misleading customers with insurance renewal notices, the insurer apologised. Now, it is defending the notices in court filings.

items.title: Global Sumud Flotilla carrying Australians intercepted on its way to Gaza by IDF
items.summary: The Global Sumud Flotilla has been intercepted by the IDF, an official tweet by the group says.

items.title: Ex-Vandemonian Touring music promoter Brendan Self charged with cocaine, cannabis offences
items.summary: The former managing director of Hobart events company Vandemonian Touring appears in court charged with trafficking cocaine and possessing cannabis.

items.title: The Friends' School denies discouraging parents from telling victims about deepfake incident
items.summary: The principal of a Tasmanian private school denies claims by two parents that the school ever tried to discourage them from telling their daughters they had been identified in AI deepfake images.

items.title: Queensland man James Lake-Kusviandy Blee on trial over Newcastle cocaine haul
items.summary: Queensland man James Lake-Kusviandy Blee is on trial over a cocaine shipment into Newcastle.

items.title: Parents of two children killed in Bowen Mountain house fire thank first responders and community
items.summary: A statement shared by the parents of two children killed in a Bowen Mountain house fire say their "family's world has been forever changed".

items.title: Paramedic stabbed in Reservoir in Melbourne's north
items.summary: Police have charged a man with attempted murder after he allegedly stabbed an on-duty paramedic in Melbourne's north.

items.title: Western Queensland's Pilungah Reserve third to gain special status ending mineral prospecting permits
items.summary: Pilungah Reserve, south-west of Mount Isa, has become Queensland's third special wildlife reserve, barring any future mining activity.

items.title: Crows' skipper Jordan Dawson ruled out for Showdown after attending brother's funeral
items.summary: Adelaide Crow's skipper Jordan Dawson has been ruled out of Friday's Showdown following his brother's sudden death.

items.title: Melbourne City to face 'team we never want to lose to' in A-League Women semifinal
items.summary: Taylor Otto sees a lot of potential in the Australian domestic league. She thinks it just needs a bigger investment to get there.

items.title: US charges Sinaloa governor over alleged ties to Sinaloa Cartel and aiding drug trafficking
items.summary: The indictment is a significant escalation of US anti-cartel action that is likely to increase tensions between the United States and Mexico.

items.title: Narrandera residents fed up with murky tap water
items.summary: When residents of Narrandera, in southern NSW, turn on the tap, the water that comes out is sometimes so dirty it stains their clothes.

items.title: Tense exchanges in court as judge turns screws on ACCC's case against Woolworths
items.summary: There have been tense exchanges in the Federal Court, with the judge overseeing the bombshell case between the ACCC and Woolworths questioning the consumer watchdog's case.

items.title: Counterterrorism intelligence's share of funding in 'significant' decline since 2020, royal commission finds
items.summary: Royal commissioner Virginia Bell says classified documents reveal a significant shift in intelligence spending away from counterterrorism since 2020, in the first findings of what failings led to last year's Bondi terror attack.

items.title: Christchurch mosque attacker's conviction appeal application declined by New Zealand court

items.title: What is inflation and should you worry about it?

items.title: Melbourne teacher charged after camera found in primary school bathroom

items.title: Keith Lees committed to stand trial over alleged 1997 murder of Meaghan Louise Rose
items.summary: Mr Lees will face a Supreme Court trial for allegedly killing Meaghan Louise Rose, whose death was originally suspected to be a suicide.

items.title: Two men denied bail after allegedly flying meth in drone in Wollongong
items.summary: Police allege a drone was used to deliver meth in Wollongong before it crashed, leading to a wider investigation.

items.summary: Perth has been blanketed with thick fog, prompting warnings for motorists to use caution in the conditions.

items.title: Does the 'Jessica' tantrum trend really work?

items.title: Wastewater data suggests meth use is at a record high. Here's why we don't need to panic
items.summary: At first glance, these findings sound alarming, but before we draw big conclusions about worsening drug use, it's worth taking a closer look at what this data can and can't tell us.

items.title: Blues out to rewrite Women's State of Origin history against Maroons in Newcastle series opener
items.summary: The Blues are yet to enjoy success in Newcastle, a fact they are mindful of ahead of the first Women's State of Origin match against the Maroons.

items.title: Police say 'timeframe of survivability' ending for missing 5yo Sharon
items.summary: After speaking to survival experts, NT police says the "timeframe of survivability" is coming to an end for a missing five-year-old girl in Alice Springs. The police commissioner also confirmed the search had been complicated by the fact the child is non-verbal.

items.title: Rare discovery of ambergris on Denmark beach excites scientists and historians
items.summary: A highly prized, rare ingredient used in the production of luxury perfumes washes up on a Denmark beach — and it comes from the intestine of sperm whales.

items.title: Donald Trump's would-be assassin took hotel selfie while 'armed to the teeth', court told
items.summary: Court documents reveal further details about the case against the alleged gunman at the White House correspondents' dinner in Washington.

items.title: Travis Head helps Sunrisers score six-wicket IPL win over Indians
items.summary: Travis Head reminds cricket fans of what he can produce with the bat, as he helps Sunrisers Hyderabad to a six-wicket win over Mumbai Indians.

items.title: NT diphtheria outbreak worsens as cases of the potentially deadly disease more than double
items.summary: Cases of a deadly, vaccine-preventable strain of diphtheria have now risen to 18, after the Centre for Disease Control first declared an outbreak last month.

items.title: Anastasia Potapova reaches Madrid Open semifinals to make WTA Tour history
items.summary: Anastasia Potapova experiences an "explosion of emotions", as her remarkable run in Madrid continues at the prestigious tournament.

items.title: New study suggests majority of regional Australians back renewable energy transition
items.summary: A new research study indicates a majority of regional Australians support the renewable transition despite some protests over planned infrastructure.

items.title: Technology should make life easier but it doesn't always for people with disability
items.summary: While the newest home technology aims to make life easier, when it prioritises aesthetics over usability, some lives are actually made much harder, writes Nas Campanella.

items.title: Will the OPEC oil cartel survive without the UAE?
items.summary: The United Arab Emirates decision to pull the plug on its OPEC membership could be the nail in the coffin for an organisation that has struggled to keep its members in line for the past 66 years.

items.title: The vine with a murky past that is strangling Vanuatu's forests
items.summary: A habitat-destroying invasive species is taking over Vanuatu's forests, but one village is fighting back and hoping to earn an income for its people at the same time.

items.title: Matthew Pavlich and Steven Baker reflect on the infamous 'Sirengate' match in Launceston 20 years on
items.summary: Two decades ago, two teams met at York Park in Launceston. The siren sounded but the match continued and the dreaded word "gate" was added to another footy scandal.

items.title: Gambling among Australian construction workers well above national average, creating safety risks
items.summary: New research reveals 89 per cent of construction workers have gambled in the past year — higher than the national average.

items.title: Flag quiz, part 1: Are you a vexillologist in the making?
items.summary: Do you know the Union Jack from the Stars and Stripes? And which flags aren't rectangular?

items.title: Lebanon ceasefire not working because free rein given to Israel, says deputy prime minister Tarek Mitri
items.summary: Lebanese Deputy Prime Minister Tarek Mitri says the government cannot exercise its authority over the country while Israel continues its bombardments and occupation.

items.title: 'Free Palestine' party pitched by YouTuber Avi Yemini to funnel votes to conservatives
items.summary: Victorian voters are being urged to look out for "misleading" party names, as right-wing activists seek to redirect progressive votes to conservative parties.

items.title: Coalition's risky Farrer strategy could clear path for One Nation
items.summary: Pauline Hanson is now set to tour more regional Coalition-held seats in her "sexy" new private plane, and the Liberal and National parties are helping clear the runway.

items.title: See how the price of everything has changed with inflation
items.summary: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.

items.title: King Charles III visits September 11 memorial as he tours New York
items.summary: King Charles III and Queen Camilla met survivors and family members of victims, as well as with New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

items.title: US Supreme Court curbs race-based voting maps in landmark ruling
items.summary: The ruling will be seen by civil rights activists as a blow and by the Republican party as a boost ahead of key midterm elections in November.

items.title: Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for flights to fundraisers and travel to catch Rinehart jet to Florida
items.summary: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for two trips that included party fundraisers, discussions with disaffected National Barnaby Joyce, a Gina Rinehart event and return flights to connect the senator to her private jet to Florida, sponsored by her major donor, Mrs Rinehart.

items.title: China inching towards resuming fuel exports as Penny Wong presses Australia's case
items.summary: Chinese state-owned oil companies have agreed to negotiate directly with Australian companies on jet fuel after the foreign minister's visit to China.

items.title: APRA music awards 2026 winners announced, with Amyl and the Sniffers taking top gong
items.summary: Punk rockers Amyl and The Sniffers have bagged three top gongs at the Australian music industry's APRA awards.

items.title: UK police declare terrorist incident after two Jewish men stabbed in London
items.summary: A man has been arrested after two men were stabbed in London in an area with a large Jewish population.

items.title: Donald Trump's criticism of Australia unfair and harmful, says US Democrat Joe Courtney
items.summary: US congressman Joe Courtney says the US president's repeated criticism of Australia is "unfair" and causing harm to the US amongst its allies.

items.title: Police explore link between alleged kidnappings and Melbourne hospitality attacks
items.summary: Police say the trio are linked to targeted attacks on promoters of a weekly event held in South Melbourne.

items.title: Fremantle Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll opens up about mental health struggles in emotional post
items.summary: Fremantle Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll reveals his mental health struggles in an emotional social media post, amid a push from players for the AFL to implement a mental health round.

items.title: SA Legislative Council result delay creating 'confusion and uncertainty'
items.summary: With less than a week until state parliament resumes, an incoming upper house member says she still cannot access the building or appoint staff because the election result is yet to be declared.

items.title: High fuel prices hurting regional families taking kids to sporting fixtures
items.summary: Country sporting clubs are rallying to help families attend fixtures as fuel prices double, with some travelling up to 640 kilometres. But it is an extra cost many will take on to maintain the sports are so integral to their communities.

items.title: Threat to homes eases as fire near Uralla in NSW downgraded
items.summary: The threat posed to homes by a grass fire burning on the state's Northern Tablelands eases after earlier prompting an Emergency alert.

items.title: FIFA allows Afghan women's football team to return to international competition
items.summary: Afghan women footballers will be able to showcase their skills to the world for the first time in five years after FIFA cleared the way for their return to international competition.

items.title: Illicit cigarettes, vapes, cash seized in raids on Canberra's black market tobacco trade
items.summary: The ACT's chief police officer wants a sustained crackdown on the black market tobacco trade, as authorities swoop on six Canberra businesses.

items.title: AFL confirms draft changes despite 'disingenuous' opposition from clubs
items.summary: The AFL is pushing ahead with draft and player-movement changes for this off-season, with Carlton president Rob Priestly and Western Bulldogs coach Luke Beveridge criticising the move.

items.title: The six weeks from hell that whipped New South Wales into State of Origin shape
items.summary: For the second year in a row both New South Wales and Queensland enter the women's State of Origin match with barely a game between them this year, but a six-week training block that one player called "one of the hardest things I've ever done" has them primed for the series opener.

items.title: Perth women share experiences of infection and surgery after nail salon visits
items.summary: More Perth women share their experience of needing surgery after having an adverse reaction to nail salon services, triggering a warning from the consumer watchdog.

items.title: Inflation details may give RBA pause for thought on interest rates
items.summary: While inflation remains well above the Reserve Bank's target, there are details in the numbers that may give it reasons to hold off another rate hike in May.
